mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals weakening labor demand and future earnings contraction. Equity investors reprice risk downward in anticipation of reduced corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2008 recovery phase with elevated unemployment at 9.5%
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market slack
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative PAYEMS changes do not precede negative SPX returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strengthening consumer demand and corporate revenue growth expectations. Equity valuations expand as investors increase earnings forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales at 362.7B in July 2010 during recovery phase
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS 3-month growth rate and subsequent 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.05 in absolute value over any 3-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS surprises fail to produce positive returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains subdued, indicating sustained monetary accommodation. This low-rate environment compresses equity risk premiums and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.18% and CPIAUCSL at 217.6 in July 2010
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary accommodation regime
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of the interaction signal falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative returns in > 45% of instances when both conditions are met simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month, signaling weakening manufacturing capacity utilization and future supply-side constraints on growth. Equity investors reduce growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 92.54 in June 2010 during post-crisis recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with volatile supply dynamics
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly changes and 5-day forward returns remains below 0.02 in absolute value over any 2-year rolling window, or negative INDPRO readings fail to precede negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both accelerate simultaneously, indicating broad-based demand recovery across residential and consumer sectors. This joint signal of demand strength drives equity valuations higher through improved earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 549K units and RSAFS at 362.7B in mid-2010
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint signal falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 40% of instances when both demand indicators accelerate simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 9% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) declines, signaling deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power. Equity investors reprice earnings downward due to expected demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.5% and PAYEMS at 130.2M in July 2010
regime_hint: post-crisis labor market weakness with persistent slack
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint labor signal falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive returns in > 50% of instances when both unemployment is elevated and employment is declining.

mechanism_narrative: Real interest rates (approximated by FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation trend) turn deeply negative, creating a financial repression regime that forces equity investors into risk assets as cash returns become negative in real terms. This mechanism produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Negative real rates in 2010 post-crisis environment
regime_hint: financial repression regime with negative real rates
failure_condition: Rank IC between the real rate proxy and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative returns in > 45% of instances when real rates are deeply negative.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above its 6-month trend while payroll employment (PAYEMS) remains flat or declining, signaling productivity-driven growth without labor demand. This supply-side strength without wage pressure supports profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2008 recovery characterized by productivity gains with labor hoarding
regime_hint: productivity-driven recovery with labor market slack
failure_condition: Rank IC of the productivity signal falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative returns in > 45% of instances when industrial production accelerates while employment growth remains subdued.
